dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Another rejection. Another email telling me I’m not good enough. Seven years. Seven years since college, and what do I have to show for it?

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: A mountain of debt. A landlord breathing down my neck. My girlfriend left me for a 'more stable future.' My friends… they don’t even answer my calls anymore.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Pathetic. That’s what I am. A pathetic excuse for a human being.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Choi Yi-Jae stood on the rooftop, the wind whipping at his threadbare jacket. Below, the city lights blurred into an indifferent tapestry.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: There’s nothing left. No hope. No future. Just… an endless, suffocating void.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Maybe… maybe it’s better this way.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WHOOSH

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: GUST OF WIND

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CRACK

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUD

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Darkness. Then… a blinding white light. A throbbing headache. And a voice.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Ugh… where am I? Did I… survive?

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: Survive? No, Choi Yi-Jae. You most certainly did not.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Who… who are you?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A figure emerged from the light. Tall, elegant, with eyes that seemed to hold the weight of eternity. She wore a simple black dress, yet exuded an aura of absolute power.

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: I am Death. The one you so casually dismissed. The one you tried to cheat.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Cheat? I… I just wanted to end it all. I was tired.

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: Tired? You called me to your side because you were 'tired'? Do you know how many souls desperately cling to life, even in their final moments? How many beg for just one more second?

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: And you, Choi Yi-Jae, you threw it away. You treated life, my domain, with utter contempt.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: I… I didn’t mean to disrespect you. I just… I couldn’t endure it anymore.

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: Endure? Life is a privilege, not a burden to be discarded at a whim. For your arrogance, for your dismissive attitude towards the sacred gift of existence, you will be punished.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Punished? What kind of punishment?

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: You will experience death, over and over again. Twelve times. You will inhabit the bodies of twelve different people on the verge of death. And you will die their deaths.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Twelve… different people? Die their deaths? What is this, some kind of sick game?

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: Indeed. It is my game. And there is one rule: if you manage to survive any of these twelve deaths, you will be granted the right to live out that person's remaining life.

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: But fail, and you will simply move on to the next. Until all twelve chances are exhausted. Then, your soul will be cast into the deepest pit of hell, never to reincarnate, never to find peace.

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: Survive… death? That’s impossible!

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: That is your challenge, Choi Yi-Jae. To truly understand the value of life, you must first truly understand the terror of death.

dialogue.speaker: DEATH
dialogue.line: Your first life… awaits.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WHOOSH

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: No! Wait! I don’t want this! I just wanted it to end!

dialogue.speaker: CHOI YI-JAE
dialogue.line: This isn’t what I wanted! This is worse!

summary: Desperate and jobless, Choi Yi-Jae ends his life by jumping from a building. He awakens in a mysterious limbo where he encounters Death, who punishes him for his casual disregard for life by forcing him to experience death twelve more times.
